questions.question: Define the exponential distribution. Derive its mean and variance.
keyPoints: State PDF: f(x) = λe^(-λx) for x ≥ 0
Verify it is a valid PDF by showing integral equals 1
Derive E(X) = 1/λ using integration by parts
Compute E(X²) = 2/λ² using integration by parts
Derive Var(X) = E(X²) - [E(X)]² = 1/λ²

questions.question: State and prove the memoryless property of the exponential distribution.
keyPoints: State: P(X > s+t | X > s) = P(X > t) for s, t ≥ 0
Write conditional probability formula
Use survival function P(X > x) = e^(-λx)
Show P(X > s+t)/P(X > s) = e^(-λ(s+t))/e^(-λs) = e^(-λt) = P(X > t)
Explain physical interpretation: no aging effect

questions.question: The time between arrivals of buses at a stop follows an exponential distribution with a mean of 15 minutes. Find: (a) the probability of waiting more than 20 minutes, (b) the probability of waiting between 10 and 20 minutes, (c) given that you have already waited 10 minutes, the probability of waiting at least 5 more minutes.
keyPoints: Compute λ = 1/15 per minute
(c) Apply memoryless property: P(X > 15 | X > 10) = P(X > 5) = e^(-5/15) = e^(-1/3)

questions.question: Explain the relationship between the exponential distribution and the Poisson distribution with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Poisson counts events in a fixed interval; Exponential measures time between events
If N(t) ~ Poisson(λt), then inter-arrival times ~ Exp(λ)
Provide a real-world example (e.g., customer arrivals)
Show how the two parameters are related
Mention that both arise from the Poisson process

questions.question: Obtain the CDF and moment generating function of the exponential distribution. Also find the median of the distribution.
keyPoints: CDF: F(x) = 1 - e^(-λx) by integrating the PDF
MGF: M_X(t) = E(e^(tX)) = λ/(λ - t) for t < λ
Derive MGF by computing the integral
Median: solve F(m) = 0.5, gives m = ln(2)/λ
Compare median with mean: median < mean (right-skewed distribution)
